revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:火守神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Homori Shrine | image = File:Homori shrine torii NO.1.jpg | location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|Izumo, Shimane|qid=Q825166|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, Unatechō 1315 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.335306|N|132.802042|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Shimane | deity = {{ill|Kushiyatama|ja|櫛八玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushiyatama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|旧郷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | Established = Unknown | Annual Festival = 2nd Sunday of October | Rituals = {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|qid=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}}, {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|fr|Reisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}}, {{ill|Kenbokukai|ja|献穀祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenbokukai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Lion dance|en|Lion dance|qid=Q713866|lt=Shishimai|1=Lion dance}}, {{ill|Kamiyo Kagura|ja|神代神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyo Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = {{ill|Izumo|qid=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} }}
[[File:Homori shrine honden.jpg|thumb|Main Hall of Homori Shrine]] '''Homori Shrine''' (火守神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|Izumo, Shimane|qid=Q825166|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kushiyatama|ja|櫛八玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushiyatama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, regarded as the ancestral deity of cuisine.
== Overview == The shrine stands along a city road branching from {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Route 26|ja|島根県道26号出雲三刀屋線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane Prefectural Route 26|1=Shimane Prefectural Road Route 26|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11476639}}, near the {{ill|Hii River|en|Hii River|qid=Q11500714|lt=Hiikawa River|1=Hii River}}. Surrounded by rural landscapes, its founding date is unknown. It is mentioned as "Homori Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, indicating its existence predates this record. Tradition states that it was originally known as "Hisusu Shrine," located near the Hisusu Falls basin beneath the estates of Unate Village. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q117415390}}, it was revered by the ruling Koshi clan. The shrine’s lion dance (Ise-style, ten steps) was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q2901957|lt=Intangible Cultural Property|1=Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)}} by Izumo City in 1959. In March 1973, a divine spirit of Kushiyatama was enshrined at {{ill|Hida Takasaki Shrine|ja|飛騨高崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hida Takasaki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Gero Onsen|ja|下呂温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gero Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, as the ancestral deity of cuisine.<ref group="source">Excerpt from Homori Shrine’s brief history.</ref>
== Enshrined Deities == '''Main deity''' * {{ill|Kushiyatama|ja|櫛八玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushiyatama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : According to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, Kushiyatama served as the chef of the Great Shrine in Tagaeshi, Izumo Province (present-day {{ill|Izumo-taisha|en|Izumo-taisha|qid=Q696362|lt=Izumo-taisha|1=Izumo-taisha}}), crafting earthenware, producing fire, and preparing divine offerings to entertain envoys and ensure successful negotiations. Hence, he is venerated as the ancestral deity of cooking, attracting many worshippers from the food industry nationwide. He is also enshrined at {{ill|Minato Shrine (Izumo-taisha)|ja|湊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato Shrine (Izumo-taisha)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, an auxiliary shrine of Izumo-taisha.
'''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} – Deities of {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|qid=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine|1=Kumano shrine}}, worshiped as local tutelary gods before the Meiji era. * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Deities of {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|qid=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine}}. * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} – deity of {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|qid=Q3471785|lt=Asama Shrine|1=Sengen shrine}}. * {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Mitoshi-no-Kami|ja|御歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitoshi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|1=Toshigami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3028663}}, Wakatokoshi-no-Kami – Deities of {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大歳神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|qq=}}, revered with annual rituals on the 7th day of the 2nd lunar month. Founded in 1927 (Shōwa 2). * {{ill|Takaokami|ja|高龗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神|1=Kuraokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q644201}}, {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|qid=Q644201|lt=Okami|1=Kuraokami}} – Water deities governing culinary water and rainfall for crops.
== Shrine Buildings == The current shrine buildings date back to {{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|qid=Q1195131|lt=Kansei|1=Kansei}} 7 (1795). The oldest surviving ridge tag (棟札) dates to {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 3 (1576).
== Auxiliary Shrines == '''Akiba Shrine''' * Enshrined deity: {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|迦具土命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
'''Wakamiya''' * Enshrined Deities: {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|布津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}} (Deities of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|qid=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine|1=Kashima Jingū}} and {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|qid=Q372380|lt=Katori Shrine|1=Katori Jingū}})
'''Inari Shrine''' * Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}}
'''Misaki Shrine''' * Enshrines guardian deities that ward off malevolent spirits from entering the village.
'''Tenmangū''' * Enshrined deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}
<gallery> File:Homori shrine akibayashiro wakayashiro.jpg|Akiba Shrine and Wakamiya File:Homori shrine inari yashiro.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Homori shrine misaki yashiro.jpg|Misaki Shrine File:Homori shrine tenmangu.jpg|Tenmangū </gallery>
== Hōchōzuka (Knife Mound) == The shrine features a {{ill|knife mound|ja|包丁塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/knife mound|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dedicated to honoring the spirits of retired knives used in culinary practices.
<gallery> File:Homori shrine hochozuka.jpg|Knife Mound </gallery>
== References == '''Sources''' {{Reflist|group=source}}
{{Shinto Sidebar}}
